gaps: No test covers DFU failure at >90% completion with firmware version reversion after device restart — existing DFU tests focus on UI elements, blocking conditions (active call), and multi-component updates, but none test the specific failure mode where the update appears to progress past 90% then fails, causing the firmware to revert to the previous version.

No test covers language change failure at >90% completion where the language briefly applies then reverts after device restart — existing language change tests (66363556, 66363557, 66363558, 66363583) cover DFU-triggered language changes, multi-component failures, and earbud disconnection scenarios, but none test the specific case where the language update progresses past 90% then fails and reverts.

No test covers the intermittent nature of DFU or language change failures — the bug scenario requires repeating steps 4-12 multiple times to confirm intermittent behavior, which is a reliability/stress testing gap not covered by any existing test case.

No test verifies the app's post-failure state display — the bug notes that after a failed DFU past 90%, the device may show as 'Up to date' in the app, which is a misleading UI state. No existing test validates the app's firmware version display accuracy after a failed/interrupted DFU.

No test covers the device state transition during the critical >90% phase of a DFU — existing tests cover blocking conditions (active call, earbud disconnected) but not the scenario where the update proceeds past 90% and then fails, which is a distinct failure mode likely related to the final write/commit phase of the firmware update process.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-FAIL-001: DFU Failure at >90% — Firmware Version Reversion After Device Restart
steps: 1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to the Poly device (e.g., Poly Sync 20, Voyager 5200, or Poly Studio E60).
2. Navigate to Device → Software & Updates and note the current firmware version displayed.
3. Tap 'Check for Updates' and confirm a firmware update is available.
4. Tap 'Update Now' to begin the firmware update process.
5. Monitor the update progress bar on the Software & Updates screen.

6. When the progress bar reaches 90-95%, perform ONE of the following actions to simulate a failure at the critical write phase:
   a) Force-kill the Poly Studio Mobile app via phone settings (Settings → Apps → Poly Studio Mobile → Force Stop), OR
   b) Toggle Bluetooth OFF on the phone for 10 seconds, then ON, OR
   c) Place an outgoing call from the phone.
7. Wait 2 minutes for the device to complete its internal process.
8. If the app was force-killed, reopen Poly Studio Mobile app and reconnect to the device.
9. Navigate to Device → Software & Updates.
10. Tap 'Check for Updates' again.
11. Note the firmware version displayed on the Software & Updates screen.
12. Power cycle the device: turn the device OFF, wait 10 seconds, then turn it ON.
13. Reconnect to the device in Poly Studio Mobile app.
14. Navigate to Device → Software & Updates and note the firmware version again.
15. Compare the firmware version from Step 2 (pre-update) with the versions from Steps 11 and 14.
generated_testcases.expected: The firmware version displayed in Steps 11 and 14 MUST match the pre-update firmware version from Step 2. The device MUST NOT show a newer firmware version. The app MUST NOT display 'Up to date' if the firmware version is the same as before the update attempt. The device should function normally (connect, play audio, adjust settings) after the reversion.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-FAIL-002: DFU Failure at >90% — Misleading 'Up to Date' UI State Validation
steps: 1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to the Poly device.
2. Navigate to Device → Software & Updates and record the current firmware version (e.g., '1.2.3').
3. Tap 'Check for Updates' and confirm a newer firmware version is available (e.g., '2.0.0').
4. Tap 'Update Now' to begin the firmware update.
5. When the progress bar reaches 90-95%, disconnect the device by turning off the device's power.
6. Wait 30 seconds, then turn the device back ON.
7. Reconnect to the device in Poly Studio Mobile app.
8. Navigate to Device → Software & Updates.
9. Observe the firmware version displayed and the status message shown (e.g., 'Up to date', 'Update available', or showing the version number).
10. Tap 'Check for Updates' and observe the result.
11. Navigate to Device → Device Info (or equivalent) and verify the firmware version listed there.
12. Compare all observed firmware versions and status messages against the pre-update version from Step 2.
generated_testcases.expected: The firmware version displayed in Steps 8-11 MUST match the pre-update version from Step 2 (e.g., '1.2.3'). The app MUST NOT display 'Up to date' when the firmware version is the same as before the update. The app should either show the current version number or indicate that an update is available. The 'Check for Updates' action should again find the newer firmware version (e.g., '2.0.0') available for download.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-FAIL-003: DFU Failure at >90% — Repeated Intermittent Failure Stress Test (5 Cycles)
steps: 1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to the Poly device.
2. Navigate to Device → Software & Updates and record the current firmware version.
3. Tap 'Check for Updates' and confirm a firmware update is available.
4. Tap 'Update Now' to begin the firmware update.
5. When the progress bar reaches 90-95%, force-kill the Poly Studio Mobile app.
6. Wait 1 minute.
7. Reopen Poly Studio Mobile app and reconnect to the device.
8. Navigate to Device → Software & Updates and verify the firmware version reverted to the pre-update version.
9. Repeat Steps 3-8 for a total of 5 cycles.
10. After the 5th cycle, power cycle the device (OFF → 10s wait → ON).
11. Reconnect in Poly Studio Mobile app.
12. Navigate to Device → Software & Updates and verify the firmware version.
13. Navigate to Audio Settings and verify all settings are functional: adjust ANC Level, Transparency Mode, and EQ preset.
14. Make a test call and verify audio works (microphone and speaker).
15. Play media audio and verify playback.
generated_testcases.expected: After each of the 5 cycles, the firmware version MUST revert to the pre-update version. After the final cycle and device restart, the firmware version must still match the pre-update version. The device must remain fully functional: audio settings adjustable and persisted, call audio working, media playback working. The device must not enter a bricked or unresponsive state after repeated failed DFU attempts.

generated_testcases.title: TC-LANG-FAIL-001: Language Change Failure at >90% — Language Reverts After Device Restart
steps: 1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to the Poly device.
2. Navigate to Device → Device Settings → Language (or equivalent language selection screen).
3. Note the currently active language (e.g., 'English').
4. Select a different language from the list (e.g., 'Deutsch' / German).
5. Tap 'Apply' or 'Confirm' to begin the language update.
6. Monitor the progress indicator on the language update screen.
7. When the progress reaches 90-95%, force-kill the Poly Studio Mobile app.
8. Wait 30 seconds.
9. Reopen Poly Studio Mobile app and reconnect to the device.
10. Navigate to Device → Device Settings → Language.
11. Observe the language displayed as active.
12. Power cycle the device: turn OFF → wait 10 seconds → turn ON.
13. Reconnect in Poly Studio Mobile app.
14. Navigate to Device → Device Settings → Language and observe the active language.
15. Use the device for 2-3 minutes: play audio, adjust volume, make a test call, and listen for voice prompts in the device's language.
16. After 2-3 minutes of use, navigate back to Device → Device Settings → Language and observe the active language again.
generated_testcases.expected: After the force-kill (Step 7-11), the language may temporarily show the new language (e.g., 'Deutsch') or the original language. After device restart (Steps 12-14), the language MUST revert to the original pre-change language (e.g., 'English'). After 2-3 minutes of use (Steps 15-16), the language must remain reverted to the original language. Voice prompts from the device must be in the original language, not the attempted new language.

generated_testcases.title: TC-LANG-FAIL-002: Language Change Failure at >90% — Intermittent Failure Stress Test (3 Cycles)
steps: 1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to the Poly device.
2. Navigate to Device → Device Settings → Language and note the current language (e.g., 'English').
3. Select a different language (e.g., 'Français' / French) and tap 'Apply'.
4. When the progress reaches 90-95%, toggle Bluetooth OFF on the phone for 15 seconds, then ON.
5. Wait 30 seconds for the device to reconnect.
6. Open Poly Studio Mobile app and navigate to Device → Device Settings → Language.
7. Note the active language displayed.
8. Power cycle the device: OFF → 10s → ON.
9. Reconnect and navigate to Device → Device Settings → Language.
10. Note the active language.
11. Repeat Steps 3-10 for a total of 3 cycles, alternating the target language each cycle (e.g., Cycle 1: French, Cycle 2: Spanish, Cycle 3: German).
12. After the 3rd cycle, use the device for 5 minutes: play media, make a call, adjust volume, listen for voice prompts.
13. After 5 minutes, navigate to Device → Device Settings → Language and note the active language.
generated_testcases.expected: After each cycle, following device restart (Step 8-9), the language MUST revert to the original language from Step 2 (e.g., 'English'). The language must NOT persist as any of the attempted languages (French, Spanish, German) after any device restart. After 5 minutes of use (Steps 12-13), the language must remain as the original language. Voice prompts must be in the original language throughout the test.

generated_testcases.title: TC-LANG-FAIL-003: Language Change Failure at >90% — App UI State vs Device Actual State Mismatch
steps: 1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to the Poly device.
2. Navigate to Device → Device Settings → Language and set the device to 'English' (confirm via voice prompt).
3. Select 'Deutsch' (German) and tap 'Apply'.
4. When the progress reaches 90-95%, force-kill the Poly Studio Mobile app.
5. Wait 30 seconds.
6. Reopen Poly Studio Mobile app and reconnect to the device.
7. Navigate to Device → Device Settings → Language and observe what language is shown as selected/highlighted in the UI.
8. WITHOUT changing any setting, power cycle the device: OFF → 10s → ON.
9. Reconnect in Poly Studio Mobile app.
10. Navigate to Device → Device Settings → Language and observe what language is shown as selected.
11. Trigger a voice prompt (e.g., press the Bluetooth button on the device and listen for the pairing prompt).
12. Note the language of the voice prompt heard from the device.
13. Compare the app UI language selection from Steps 7 and 10 with the actual device voice prompt language from Step 12.
generated_testcases.expected: After the force-kill (Step 7), the app UI may incorrectly show 'Deutsch' as selected. After device restart (Steps 8-10), the app UI MUST show 'English' as the selected language. The voice prompt from Step 12 MUST be in English, confirming the device's actual language is English. The app UI must not persistently show a language that does not match the device's actual language. This validates that the app correctly refreshes its language state from the device after a restart.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-FAIL-004: DFU Failure at >90% — Device State Transition During Critical Write Phase
steps: 1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to the Poly device.
2. Navigate to Device → Software & Updates and record the current firmware version.
3. Tap 'Check for Updates' and confirm a newer firmware version is available.
4. Tap 'Update Now' to begin the firmware update.
5. When the progress bar reaches 90-95%, perform the following action: press and hold the device's power button for 10 seconds to force a device power-off.
6. Wait 30 seconds.
7. Power ON the device by pressing the power button.
8. Open Poly Studio Mobile app and attempt to reconnect to the device.
9. Navigate to Device → Software & Updates and observe the firmware version displayed.
10. Tap 'Check for Updates' and observe the result.
11. Navigate to Device → Device Info and verify the firmware version.
12. Test basic device functionality: play media audio, adjust volume using the device's volume buttons, test the mute function.
13. Navigate to Audio Settings → ANC Level and adjust the slider to 50, then verify the change is applied.
14. Navigate to Audio Settings → Transparency Mode → Environment and set Surround level to 75, then verify.
generated_testcases.expected: The firmware version in Steps 9-11 MUST match the pre-update version from Step 2. The device must successfully reconnect to the app after the forced power-off during the DFU critical phase. All basic functionality (media playback, volume control, mute) must work normally. Audio settings (ANC Level, Transparency Surround level) must be adjustable and persist after being set. The device must not be bricked or stuck in a boot loop after the forced power-off during the DFU write phase.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-FAIL-005: DFU Failure at >90% — Bluetooth Disconnect During Critical Phase with Multi-Device Pairing
steps: 1. Pair the Poly device with Phone A (primary) and Phone B (secondary).
2. On Phone A, open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to the device.
3. Navigate to Device → Software & Updates and record the current firmware version.
4. Tap 'Check for Updates' and confirm a newer firmware version is available.
5. Tap 'Update Now' to begin the firmware update.
6. When the progress bar reaches 90-95%, on Phone B, initiate a Bluetooth connection to the Poly device (simulating a multi-device switching event during the critical write phase).
7. Wait 1 minute.
8. On Phone A, observe the Poly Studio Mobile app UI — note any error messages, disconnection alerts, or progress bar state.
9. Close and reopen Poly Studio Mobile app on Phone A.
10. Attempt to reconnect to the device from Phone A.
11. Navigate to Device → Software & Updates and note the firmware version.
12. Tap 'Check for Updates' and observe the result.
13. Power cycle the device: OFF → 10s → ON.
14. Reconnect from Phone A and navigate to Device → Software & Updates.
15. Note the firmware version again.
16. Verify the device can still connect to both Phone A and Phone B for audio.
generated_testcases.expected: The firmware version in Steps 11, 14, and 15 MUST match the pre-update version from Step 3. The app on Phone A should show a disconnection or update failure notification after the multi-device switching event. The device must remain functional and connectable to both paired phones. The 'Check for Updates' action must again find the newer firmware version available. The device must not show any signs of corruption (e.g., unable to pair, unable to play audio, distorted audio).
